libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Prefer in-process tests around CacheProperties, CacheManagerCustomizers, CacheMetricsRegistrar where applicable, and CacheAutoConfiguration with an @EnableCaching user configuration. The simple/concurrent-map/no-op/generic cache paths can be covered without Redis, Couchbase, Hazelcast, Infinispan, JCache, Caffeine, or Cache2k services.

deterministic_setup.reason: spring-boot-cache contains @AutoConfiguration classes and imports condition annotations/classes from spring-boot-autoconfigure, but that dependency is optional in the module source and not present in the published runtime dependency set.

Spring Boot's own tests for cache auto-configuration use ApplicationContextRunner; this is the practical in-process way to exercise CacheAutoConfiguration without starting a full application or external cache service.

risks: Provider-specific paths are guarded by optional classpath conditions; tests that directly target Redis, Couchbase, Hazelcast, Infinispan, JCache, Caffeine, Cache2k, or actuator endpoint integrations may need additional provider dependencies and should not be attempted unless deliberately added.
No Docker image is needed for meaningful baseline cache auto-configuration coverage; external cache services would only be needed for integration-style provider tests.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a class-bearing Spring Boot cache auto-configuration module. Its published runtime dependencies are only spring-boot and spring-context-support, but meaningful coverage of its central auto-configuration path requires Spring Boot's auto-configuration and test context runner support.
